entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench, the crop used for the protoplast regeneration study

entities.name: PLANT REGENERATION
entities.description: The process of developing whole plants from mesophyll-derived protoplasts

entities.name: PROTOPLASTS
entities.description: Plant cells with cell walls removed, used as the starting material for regeneration

entities.name: MESOPHYLL
entities.description: The leaf tissue used as the source for isolating protoplasts

entities.name: GENOTYPE 296B
entities.description: A specific sorghum genotype that showed higher responsiveness in protoplast yield and regeneration

entities.name: GENOTYPE IS 32266
entities.description: A specific sorghum genotype used for comparison in the regeneration study

entities.name: PLATING EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The percentage of protoplasts that divide and form colonies

entities.name: REGENERATION FREQUENCY
entities.description: The percentage of microcalli that successfully regenerate into whole plants

entities.name: MS MEDIUM
entities.description: Murashige and Skoog medium used for the culture of microcalli

entities.name: KINETIN
entities.description: A cytokinin used in the MS medium to promote plant regeneration

entities.name: BAP
entities.description: 6-Benzylaminopurine, a cytokinin used in the MS medium to promote plant regeneration

entities.name: GLASSHOUSE
entities.description: The controlled environment where regenerated plants were first established

entities.name: FIELD
entities.description: The outdoor environment where plants were grown to maturity

entities.name: DARKNESS
entities.description: The condition of keeping plants in the dark for 2 days prior to harvesting leaves
